description: A plain macro/scientific specimen photograph (800x450) of an insect, accompanying a CBP agriculture-interdiction story about a 'first in nation' pest interception. The image is a sharp, high-magnification side-profile of a snout beetle (a weevil) set against a solid black background; the photo shows its mottled brown-and-tan scaled body, segmented legs, antennae, and the characteristic elongated snout/rostrum and dark eye. The lighting is studio macro photography emphasizing texture and detail. There is no graphic-design overlay, text, caption, slogan, or agency seal applied to the image — it is a straightforward documentary/specimen photo (the kind of reference image used to illustrate an intercepted agricultural pest). The news framing (San Juan CBP officers and an agriculture specialist finding the pest on an aircraft from Senegal) is provided by the tweet, not the picture.
